structData.title: Budget exhaustion — campaign stops delivering before end of day
structData.content:
Budget exhaustion occurs when a campaign spends its full daily budget before the end of the day, causing delivery to stop completely for the remaining hours. This is one of the most common causes of campaigns appearing to be broken when they are actually working as configured.

HOW TO DIAGNOSE
Call getHourlySpendCurve with days=3. Look for:
  budgetHit: true  — means budget ran out that day
  budgetHitTime    — the exact time delivery stopped, e.g. 08:03 AM
  pattern field    — if it says 'front-loaded', ASAP pacing is the cause

Also call getPacingSettings. If pacingMode is ASAP, that is the root cause.

ASAP PACING — HOW IT CAUSES EXHAUSTION
ASAP pacing instructs the DSP to spend the daily budget as fast as possible. Morning hours (7 AM–11 AM) have the highest traffic volume in most markets. An ASAP-paced campaign will saturate this window and exhaust the budget, leaving no spend for afternoon and evening.

PACING MODES EXPLAINED
  ASAP: Spend as fast as possible. Budget exhausts early. Use only for time-sensitive promotions.
  EVEN: Distribute budget evenly across 24 hours. Divides dailyBudget by 24 to set an hourly cap. Prevents morning exhaustion. Recommended for always-on campaigns.
  AHEAD: Slightly front-loads spending (faster in AM) but protects against evening underdelivery. Good balance between ASAP and EVEN.

HOW TO FIX
1. Change pacingMode from ASAP to EVEN in the campaign settings.
2. If budget is legitimately too low for your targeting scale, increase dailyBudget.
3. Check frequency caps — if uncapped, a small audience gets hit many times quickly burning budget. Set a cap of 2–3 impressions per user per day.
4. Review bid — a high bid wins more expensive auctions and burns budget faster. If ROAS is strong, a slight bid reduction can extend delivery.

BUDGET RESET
Daily budgets reset at midnight UTC. A campaign paused due to budget exhaustion will automatically become eligible to serve again at 00:00 UTC the next day, assuming it is still in ACTIVE status and has budget for the new day.
